metadata.llm_reasoning: Frontend depends on multiple downstream services; a fault in any of these dependencies could propagate to frontend, causing the symptom. The most plausible propagation paths are from any of the listed dependencies directly into frontend. There are no alternate structural paths shown that bypass frontend to produce the same symptom, given the topology. The blast radius centers on frontend, with the core services that frontend relies on (cartservice, productcatalogservice, checkoutservice) being the most critical to monitor due to their central role in order-facing workflows.

result.synthesis_reasoning: All three sources diverge or provide limited evidence: the Log Agent provides no observables to identify a root cause; the Topology Agent confirms plausible propagation paths into the frontend but does not identify a specific upstream failing service; the TCB-RCA Engine points to frontend as the initial candidate with no observed anomalies. Given the absence of concrete upstream error data and the imperative to respect topology while not overreaching beyond available signals, the most cautious conclusion is that the root-cause is not determinable beyond the frontend being the focal point of the incident. This aligns with a weak_evidence convergence and a low overall confidence, avoiding unjustified speculation about deeper root causes.
